# AGENTS.md — Marketing Hub (marketing-hub)

Marketing Hub — All-in-One Marketing Command Center. A Next.js + React application.

## Stack
- Next.js + React (28 dependencies)
- `next.config.js`, `components.json` (shadcn-style UI), Capacitor (`capacitor.config.json`
  → mobile packaging), Tailwind/CSS
- `data/` and `docs/` hold content and documentation

## Common commands
- `npm run dev` — local dev server
- `npm run build` — production build
- `npm run start` — serve the production build
- `npm run lint` — lint
- `npm run seed` — seed local data

## Notes for the coding agent (pi)
- pi is the coding agent for this project.

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * ICP Lead Scoring — score contacts against the Ideal Customer Profile.
3:  *
4:  * Trains on the hub's closed-won deals (the best customers) and scores every
5:  * contact against that profile. Returns a 0-1 score with fit signals + gap signals.
6:  *
7:  * Pattern looted from: GTM Engine (sohan-a11y/gtm-engine) — ICP Agent.
8:  * Reconciled with BAZ doctrine: revenue not vanity, proof beats promises.
9:  */
10: import { db, uid, now } from "@/lib/db";
11: 
12: export interface ICPProfile {
13:   topIndustries: { industry: string; weight: number }[];
14:   avgDealValue: number;
15:   winRateByIndustry: Record<string, number>;
16:   totalTrained: number;
17: }
18: 
19: export interface ContactScore {
20:   contactId: string;
21:   score: number;          // 0-1
22:   grade: string;          // A, B, C, D
23:   fitSignals: string[];
24:   gapSignals: string[];
25:   recommendation: string;
26: }
27: 
28: let _icpProfile: ICPProfile | null = null;
29: 
30: /**
31:  * Train the ICP from the hub's closed-won deals.
32:  * The best customers define the ideal profile.
33:  */
34: export function trainICP(): ICPProfile {
35:   if (_icpProfile) return _icpProfile;
36: 
37:   // Get all closed-won deals with their contacts
38:   const wonDeals = db.prepare(`
39:     SELECT d.value, c.industry, c.company, c.title
40:     FROM deals d
41:     JOIN contacts c ON d.contact_id = c.id
42:     WHERE d.stage = 'closed-won'
43:   `).all() as { value: number; industry: string; company: string; title: string }[];
44: 
45:   if (wonDeals.length === 0) {
46:     // No won deals yet — use all deals as a rough profile
47:     const allDeals = db.prepare(`
48:       SELECT d.value, c.industry FROM deals d
49:       JOIN contacts c ON d.contact_id = c.id WHERE d.value > 0
50:     `).all() as { value: number; industry: string }[];
51: 
52:     if (allDeals.length === 0) {
53:       _icpProfile = { topIndustries: [], avgDealValue: 0, winRateByIndustry: {}, totalTrained: 0 };
54:       return _icpProfile;
55:     }
56: 
57:     const industries = allDeals.reduce((acc, d) => {
58:       if (d.industry) acc[d.industry] = (acc[d.industry] || 0) + 1;
59:       return acc;
60:     }, {} as Record<string, number>);
61: 
62:     _icpProfile = {
63:       topIndustries: Object.entries(industries)
64:         .sort(([, a], [, b]) => b - a)
65:         .slice(0, 5)
66:         .map(([industry, count]) => ({ industry, weight: count / allDeals.length })),
67:       avgDealValue: allDeals.reduce((s, d) => s + d.value, 0) / allDeals.length,
68:       winRateByIndustry: {},
69:       totalTrained: allDeals.length,
70:     };
71:     return _icpProfile;
72:   }
73: 
74:   // Build the ICP from won deals
75:   const industries = wonDeals.reduce((acc, d) => {
76:     if (d.industry) acc[d.industry] = (acc[d.industry] || 0) + 1;
77:     return acc;
78:   }, {} as Record<string, number>);
79: 
80:   // Calculate win rate per industry (won / total deals in that industry)
81:   const allDealsByIndustry = db.prepare(`
82:     SELECT c.industry, d.stage FROM deals d
83:     JOIN contacts c ON d.contact_id = c.id WHERE c.industry IS NOT NULL
84:   `).all() as { industry: string; stage: string }[];
85: 
86:   const winRateByIndustry: Record<string, number> = {};
87:   const totalByIndustry: Record<string, number> = {};
88:   const wonByIndustry: Record<string, number> = {};
89:   for (const d of allDealsByIndustry) {
90:     totalByIndustry[d.industry] = (totalByIndustry[d.industry] || 0) + 1;
91:     if (d.stage === "closed-won") wonByIndustry[d.industry] = (wonByIndustry[d.industry] || 0) + 1;
92:   }
93:   for (const industry of Object.keys(totalByIndustry)) {
94:     winRateByIndustry[industry] = (wonByIndustry[industry] || 0) / totalByIndustry[industry];
95:   }
96: 
97:   _icpProfile = {
98:     topIndustries: Object.entries(industries)
99:       .sort(([, a], [, b]) => b - a)
100:       .slice(0, 5)
101:       .map(([industry, count]) => ({ industry, weight: count / wonDeals.length })),
102:     avgDealValue: wonDeals.reduce((s, d) => s + d.value, 0) / wonDeals.length,
103:     winRateByIndustry,
104:     totalTrained: wonDeals.length,
105:   };
106: 
107:   return _icpProfile;
108: }
109: 
110: /**
111:  * Score a single contact against the ICP.
112:  */
113: export function scoreContact(contactId: string): ContactScore {
114:   const profile = trainICP();
115:   const contact = db.prepare(`
116:     SELECT id, name, email, industry, company, title FROM contacts WHERE id = ?
117:   `).get(contactId) as { id: string; name: string; email: string; industry: string; company: string; title: string } | undefined;
118: 
119:   if (!contact) {
120:     return {
121:       contactId,
122:       score: 0,
123:       grade: "F",
124:       fitSignals: [],
125:       gapSignals: ["Contact not found"],
126:       recommendation: "Contact does not exist.",
127:     };
128:   }
129: 
130:   let score = 0.3; // base score
131:   const fitSignals: string[] = [];
132:   const gapSignals: string[] = [];
133: 
134:   // 1. Industry match (highest weight)
135:   if (contact.industry && profile.topIndustries.length > 0) {
136:     const match = profile.topIndustries.find((i) => i.industry === contact.industry);
137:     if (match) {
138:       score += match.weight * 0.4;
139:       fitSignals.push(`Industry "${contact.industry}" matches ICP (${(match.weight * 100).toFixed(0)}% of won deals)`);
140:       const winRate = profile.winRateByIndustry[contact.industry];
141:       if (winRate !== undefined && winRate > 0.5) {
142:         score += 0.1;
143:         fitSignals.push(`High win rate in ${contact.industry} (${(winRate * 100).toFixed(0)}%)`);
144:       }
145:     } else {
146:       gapSignals.push(`Industry "${contact.industry}" not in top ICP industries`);
147:     }
148:   }
149: 
150:   // 2. Deal value potential (if the contact has deals, compare to avg)
151:   const contactDeals = db.prepare(`
152:     SELECT value, stage FROM deals WHERE contact_id = ?
153:   `).all(contactId) as { value: number; stage: string }[];
154: 
155:   if (contactDeals.length > 0) {
156:     const avgContactDeal = contactDeals.reduce((s, d) => s + d.value, 0) / contactDeals.length;
157:     if (profile.avgDealValue > 0) {
158:       const ratio = avgContactDeal / profile.avgDealValue;
159:       if (ratio > 0.8) {
160:         score += 0.15;
161:         fitSignals.push(`Deal value ($${avgContactDeal.toLocaleString()}) aligns with ICP average ($${profile.avgDealValue.toLocaleString()})`);
162:       } else if (ratio < 0.3) {
163:         score -= 0.1;
164:         gapSignals.push(`Deal value ($${avgContactDeal.toLocaleString()}) below ICP average ($${profile.avgDealValue.toLocaleString()})`);
165:       }
166:     }
167:   }
168: 
169:   // 3. Title seniority (decision-makers score higher)
170:   if (contact.title) {
171:     const seniorTitles = /\b(CEO|CTO|CMO|CFO|founder|owner|vp|vice president|director|head of)\b/i;
172:     if (seniorTitles.test(contact.title)) {
173:       score += 0.1;
174:       fitSignals.push(`Senior title: ${contact.title} (decision-maker)`);
175:     }
176:   }
177: 
178:   // 4. Has company (B2B signal)
179:   if (contact.company) {
180:     score += 0.05;
181:     fitSignals.push(`Company identified: ${contact.company}`);
182:   } else {
183:     gapSignals.push("No company associated");
184:   }
185: 
186:   // Clamp 0-1
187:   score = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, score));
188: 
189:   const grade = score >= 0.8 ? "A" : score >= 0.6 ? "B" : score >= 0.4 ? "C" : "D";
190:   const recommendation =
191:     score >= 0.7 ? "Prioritize — strong ICP fit. Route to outreach." :
192:     score >= 0.5 ? "Nurture — moderate fit. Keep in sequence." :
193:     "Low priority — weak ICP fit. Reconsider or deprioritize.";
194: 
195:   return { contactId, score, grade, fitSignals, gapSignals, recommendation };
196: }
197: 
198: /**
199:  * Score all contacts + return sorted by score.
200:  */
201: export function scoreAllContacts(limit = 50): ContactScore[] {
202:   const contacts = db.prepare(`
203:     SELECT id FROM contacts ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT ?
204:   `).all(limit) as { id: string }[];
205: 
206:   return contacts
207:     .map((c) => scoreContact(c.id))
208:     .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);
209: }
210: 
211: /**
212:  * Get the ICP profile summary (for display / agent context).
213:  */
214: export function getICPSummary(): string {
215:   const profile = trainICP();
216:   if (profile.totalTrained === 0) {
217:     return "ICP: No training data yet (no closed-won deals). Score is based on rough heuristics.";
218:   }
219:   const parts = [
220:     `ICP (trained on ${profile.totalTrained} won deals):`,
221:     `- Average deal value: $${profile.avgDealValue.toLocaleString()}`,
222:     `- Top industries: ${profile.topIndustries.map((i) => `${i.industry} (${(i.weight * 100).toFixed(0)}%)`).join(", ")}`,
223:   ];
224:   const topWinRates = Object.entries(profile.winRateByIndustry)
225:     .filter(([, r]) => r > 0.5)
226:     .sort(([, a], [, b]) => b - a)
227:     .slice(0, 3);
228:   if (topWinRates.length > 0) {
229:     parts.push(`- High win-rate industries: ${topWinRates.map(([i, r]) => `${i} (${(r * 100).toFixed(0)}%)`).join(", ")}`);
230:   }
231:   return parts.join("\n");
232: }

(End of file - total 232 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * Multi-touch attribution.
3:  *
4:  * The core insight: a deal almost never closes from a single touchpoint.
5:  * "Last-touch" attribution over-credits the final channel; "first-touch"
6:  * over-credits awareness. Real attribution distributes credit.
7:  *
8:  * Models implemented:
9:  *   - first_touch   — 100% credit to the first touchpoint
10:  *   - last_touch    — 100% credit to the last touchpoint (HubSpot default)
11:  *   - linear        — equal credit across all touchpoints
12:  *   - time_decay    — exponentially more credit to recent touches (half-life 7d)
13:  *   - position_based — 40% first + 40% last + 20% spread across middle (U-shaped)
14:  *   - data_driven   — heuristic: channels with >50% conversion rate get more credit
15:  */
16: import { db } from "@/lib/db";
17: 
18: export type AttributionModel =
19:   "first_touch" | "last_touch" | "linear" | "time_decay" | "position_based" | "data_driven";
20: 
21: export interface Touchpoint {
22:   id: string;
23:   deal_id: string;
24:   contact_id?: string;
25:   channel: string;
26:   campaign_id?: string;
27:   asset?: string;
28:   occurred_at: number;
29: }
30: 
31: export interface ChannelCredit {
32:   channel: string;
33:   credit: number; // absolute $ amount
34:   share: number; // 0..1 of total
35:   touchpoint_count: number;
36: }
37: 
38: export interface DealAttribution {
39:   deal_id: string;
40:   deal_title: string;
41:   deal_value: number;
42:   model: AttributionModel;
43:   touchpoints: number;
44:   credits: ChannelCredit[];
45:   first_touch_channel: string;
46:   last_touch_channel: string;
47:   days_to_close: number;
48: }
49: 
50: const VALID_MODELS: AttributionModel[] = [
51:   "first_touch",
52:   "last_touch",
53:   "linear",
54:   "time_decay",
55:   "position_based",
56:   "data_driven",
57: ];
58: 
59: function getTouchpointsForDeal(dealId: string): Touchpoint[] {
60:   return db
61:     .prepare(`SELECT * FROM touchpoints WHERE deal_id = ? ORDER BY occurred_at ASC`)
62:     .all(dealId) as Record<string, unknown>[];
63: }
64: 
65: function getConversionRateByChannel(): Record<string, number> {
66:   // Data-driven prior: ratio of touches that resulted in a won deal
67:   const rows = db
68:     .prepare(
69:       `
70:     SELECT t.channel,
71:            COUNT(DISTINCT t.id) AS touches,
72:            COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN d.stage = 'won' THEN d.id END) AS won_deals
73:     FROM touchpoints t
74:     LEFT JOIN deals d ON d.id = t.deal_id
75:     GROUP BY t.channel
76:   `,
77:     )
78:     .all() as Record<string, unknown>[];
79:   const out: Record<string, number> = {};
80:   for (const r of rows) {
81:     out[r.channel] = r.touches > 0 ? r.won_deals / r.touches : 0;
82:   }
83:   return out;
84: }
85: 
86: export function attributeDeal(
87:   dealId: string,
88:   model: AttributionModel = "position_based",
89: ): DealAttribution | null {
90:   if (!VALID_MODELS.includes(model)) return null;
91:   const deal = db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM deals WHERE id = ?`).get(dealId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
92:   if (!deal) return null;
93:   const tps = getTouchpointsForDeal(dealId);
94:   if (!tps.length) {
95:     return {
96:       deal_id: dealId,
97:       deal_title: deal.title,
98:       deal_value: deal.value || 0,
99:       model,
100:       touchpoints: 0,
101:       credits: [],
102:       first_touch_channel: "—",
103:       last_touch_channel: "—",
104:       days_to_close: deal.won_at ? Math.floor((deal.won_at - deal.created_at) / 86400000) : 0,
105:     };
106:   }
107: 
108:   const value = deal.value || 0;
109:   let weights: number[];
110: 
111:   switch (model) {
112:     case "first_touch":
113:       weights = tps.map((_, i) => (i === 0 ? 1 : 0));
114:       break;
115:     case "last_touch":
116:       weights = tps.map((_, i) => (i === tps.length - 1 ? 1 : 0));
117:       break;
118:     case "linear":
119:       weights = tps.map(() => 1 / tps.length);
120:       break;
121:     case "time_decay": {
122:       const halfLife = 7 * 86400000;
123:       const last = Math.max(...tps.map((t) => t.occurred_at));
124:       weights = tps.map((t) => Math.pow(0.5, (last - t.occurred_at) / halfLife));
125:       const wsum = weights.reduce((s, w) => s + w, 0);
126:       weights = weights.map((w) => w / wsum);
127:       break;
128:     }
129:     case "position_based": {
130:       // U-shaped: 40% first + 40% last + 20% spread
131:       if (tps.length === 1) weights = [1];
132:       else if (tps.length === 2) weights = [0.5, 0.5];
133:       else {
134:         weights = tps.map(() => 0);
135:         weights[0] = 0.4;
136:         weights[weights.length - 1] = 0.4;
137:         const middle = 0.2 / (tps.length - 2);
138:         for (let i = 1; i < tps.length - 1; i++) weights[i] = middle;
139:       }
140:       break;
141:     }
142:     case "data_driven": {
143:       const rates = getConversionRateByChannel();
144:       const mean =
145:         Object.values(rates).reduce((s, r) => s + r, 0) / Math.max(1, Object.keys(rates).length);
146:       weights = tps.map((t) => Math.max(0.1, rates[t.channel] || mean));
147:       const wsum = weights.reduce((s, w) => s + w, 0);
148:       weights = weights.map((w) => w / wsum);
149:       break;
150:     }
151:   }
152: 
153:   // Aggregate by channel
154:   const channelMap = new Map<string, { credit: number; count: number }>();
155:   tps.forEach((t, i) => {
156:     const w = weights[i];
157:     const credit = value * w;
158:     const cur = channelMap.get(t.channel) || { credit: 0, count: 0 };
159:     cur.credit += credit;
160:     cur.count += 1;
161:     channelMap.set(t.channel, cur);
162:   });
163: 
164:   const credits: ChannelCredit[] = Array.from(channelMap.entries())
165:     .map(([channel, v]) => ({
166:       channel,
167:       credit: Math.round(v.credit),
168:       share: value > 0 ? v.credit / value : 0,
169:       touchpoint_count: v.count,
170:     }))
171:     .sort((a, b) => b.credit - a.credit);
172: 
173:   return {
174:     deal_id: dealId,
175:     deal_title: deal.title,
176:     deal_value: value,
177:     model,
178:     touchpoints: tps.length,
179:     credits,
180:     first_touch_channel: tps[0].channel,
181:     last_touch_channel: tps[tps.length - 1].channel,
182:     days_to_close: deal.won_at ? Math.floor((deal.won_at - deal.created_at) / 86400000) : 0,
183:   };
184: }
185: 
186: /** Attribute ALL won deals and roll up by channel. */
187: export function attributionRollup(model: AttributionModel = "position_based"): {
188:   channel_credits: ChannelCredit[];
189:   total_attributed: number;
190:   deals_attributed: number;
191:   model: AttributionModel;
192: } {
193:   const deals = db.prepare(`SELECT id FROM deals WHERE stage = 'won'`).all() as Record<string, unknown>[];
194:   const channelMap = new Map<string, number>();
195:   let totalAttributed = 0;
196:   let count = 0;
197:   for (const d of deals) {
198:     const r = attributeDeal(d.id, model);
199:     if (!r) continue;
200:     count++;
201:     totalAttributed += r.deal_value;
202:     for (const c of r.credits) {
203:       channelMap.set(c.channel, (channelMap.get(c.channel) || 0) + c.credit);
204:     }
205:   }
206:   const channel_credits: ChannelCredit[] = Array.from(channelMap.entries())
207:     .map(([channel, credit]) => ({
208:       channel,
209:       credit: Math.round(credit),
210:       share: totalAttributed > 0 ? credit / totalAttributed : 0,
211:       touchpoint_count: 0,
212:     }))
213:     .sort((a, b) => b.credit - a.credit);
214:   return { channel_credits, total_attributed: totalAttributed, deals_attributed: count, model };
215: }
216: 
217: export function logTouchpoint(t: Omit<Touchpoint, "id">): Touchpoint {
218:   const id = `tp_${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 10)}${Date.now().toString(36)}`;
219:   db.prepare(
220:     `
221:     INSERT INTO touchpoints (id, deal_id, contact_id, channel, campaign_id, asset, occurred_at, attribution_weight, created_at)
222:     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 1.0, ?)
223:   `,
224:   ).run(
225:     id,
226:     t.deal_id,
227:     t.contact_id || null,
228:     t.channel,
229:     t.campaign_id || null,
230:     t.asset || null,
231:     t.occurred_at,
232:     Date.now(),
233:   );
234:   return { ...t, id };
235: }

(End of file - total 235 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
/**
 * Multi-touch attribution.
 *
 * The core insight: a deal almost never closes from a single touchpoint.
 * "Last-touch" attribution over-credits the final channel; "first-touch"
 * over-credits awareness. Real attribution distributes credit.
 *
 * Models implemented:
 *   - first_touch   — 100% credit to the first touchpoint
 *   - last_touch    — 100% credit to the last touchpoint (HubSpot default)
 *   - linear        — equal credit across all touchpoints
 *   - time_decay    — exponentially more credit to recent touches (half-life 7d)
 *   - position_based — 40% first + 40% last + 20% spread across middle (U-shaped)
 *   - data_driven   — heuristic: channels with >50% conversion rate get more credit
 */
import { db } from "@/lib/db";

export type AttributionModel =
  "first_touch" | "last_touch" | "linear" | "time_decay" | "position_based" | "data_driven";

1: /**
2:  * Quality gate — layer 4 of the 5-layer agent architecture.
3:  *
4:  * Every agent output passes through this gate BEFORE it's returned to the user.
5:  * Checks: no hype words, revenue-anchored, no fabricated metrics, proof density,
6:  * brand voice. Returns a score (0-100, A+–F) + issues + the cleaned text.
7:  *
8:  * Pattern looted from: DM Pro's eval/QA layer (hallucination detection, claim
9:  * verification, A+–F grading), Kai's Four U's quality gate, Anyword's predictive
10:  * scoring. Reconciled with BAZ doctrine: no hype, revenue not vanity, proof beats
11:  * promises.
12:  */
13: 
14: // The BAZ doctrine banned-word list (hype that violates the brand voice)
15: const HYPE_WORDS = [
16:   "unshakeable", "absolute", "lethal", "dominance", "world-class",
17:   "cutting-edge", "innovative", "game-changing", "unlock your potential",
18:   "revolutionary", "disruptive", "synergy", "ninja", "best-in-class",
19:   "passionate", "dedicated", "empower", "seamless", "robust",
20: ];
21: 
22: // Revenue-anchoring keywords (at least one should appear in a strategic output)
23: const REVENUE_KEYWORDS = [
24:   "revenue", "pipeline", "LTV", "CAC", "payback", "ROI",
25:   "conversion", "close rate", "churn", "MRR", "ARR",
26:   "profit", "margin", "cost", "budget", "forecast",
27: ];
28: 
29: export interface QualityResult {
30:   passed: boolean;
31:   score: number;          // 0-100
32:   grade: string;          // A+, A, B, C, D, F
33:   issues: string[];       // specific problems found
34:   cleanedText: string;    // the text with hype words flagged
35:   metrics: {
36:     hypeWordCount: number;
37:     revenueAnchored: boolean;
38:     fabricatedMetrics: string[];
39:     proofDensity: number;  // 0-1 (claims with proof / total claims)
40:     // Four U's scoring (looted from Kai CMO Harness)
41:     fourUs: {
42:       usefulness: number;     // 0-1
43:       urgency: number;        // 0-1
44:       uniqueness: number;     // 0-1
45:       ultraSpecificity: number; // 0-1
46:       average: number;        // 0-1
47:     };
48:     // Hallucination detection (looted from DM Pro eval/QA)
49:     hallucinationFlags: string[];
50:     suspiciousUrls: string[];
51:     unsubstantiatedClaims: number;
52:   };
53: }
54: 
55: /**
56:  * Run the quality gate on agent output.
57:  * @param text The LLM-generated output text.
58:  * @param hubMetrics The real hub metrics (to verify against — no fabrication).
59:  */
60: export function qualityGate(text: string, hubMetrics?: Record<string, number | string>): QualityResult {
61:   const issues: string[] = [];
62:   const lower = text.toLowerCase();
63:   let score = 100;
64: 
65:   // 1. No hype words
66:   const foundHype = HYPE_WORDS.filter((w) => lower.includes(w));
67:   if (foundHype.length > 0) {
68:     score -= foundHype.length * 10;
69:     issues.push(`Hype words found: ${foundHype.join(", ")} (BAZ doctrine: no hype)`);
70:   }
71: 
72:   // 2. Revenue-anchored (at least one revenue keyword in strategic output)
73:   const revenueFound = REVENUE_KEYWORDS.some((k) => lower.includes(k.toLowerCase()));
74:   if (!revenueFound) {
75:     score -= 15;
76:     issues.push("Output is not revenue-anchored (no LTV/CAC/payback/pipeline mention). BAZ doctrine: revenue not vanity.");
77:   }
78: 
79:   // 3. No fabricated metrics (flag numbers not in the hub data)
80:   const fabricatedMetrics: string[] = [];
81:   if (hubMetrics) {
82:     // Extract numbers from the text (e.g., "$50,000", "23%", "12 deals")
83:     const numberPatterns = text.match(/\$[\d,]+|\d+%|\d+\s*(?:deals?|contacts?|campaigns?|customers?|clients?)/gi) || [];
84:     for (const pattern of numberPatterns) {
85:       const num = parseInt(pattern.replace(/[^0-9]/g, ""), 10);
86:       if (isNaN(num)) continue;
87: 
88:       // Check if this number matches any real hub metric
89:       const matchesHubMetric = Object.values(hubMetrics).some((v) => {
90:         if (typeof v === "number") return v === num;
91:         return false;
92:       });
93:       if (!matchesHubMetric && num > 0 && num < 1000000) {
94:         // Flag suspicious numbers (not in the hub data, not a percentage, not a generic small number)
95:         if (!pattern.includes("%") && num > 10) {
96:           fabricatedMetrics.push(pattern);
97:         }
98:       }
99:     }
100:     if (fabricatedMetrics.length > 0) {
101:       score -= fabricatedMetrics.length * 15;
102:       issues.push(`Potential fabricated metrics (not in hub data): ${fabricatedMetrics.join(", ")}. BAZ doctrine: proof beats promises — no fabricated numbers.`);
103:     }
104:   }
105: 
106:   // 4. Proof density (rough heuristic: sentences with "because", "due to", "from", "result", "data" = proof-backed)
107:   const sentences = text.split(/[.!?]+/).filter((s) => s.trim().length > 20);
108:   const proofSentences = sentences.filter((s) =>
109:     /because|due to|from|result|data|measured|tracked|attributed|according|based on/i.test(s),
110:   );
111:   const proofDensity = sentences.length > 0 ? proofSentences.length / sentences.length : 0;
112:   if (proofDensity < 0.2 && sentences.length > 3) {
113:     score -= 10;
114:     issues.push(`Low proof density (${(proofDensity * 100).toFixed(0)}% of claims have reasoning). BAZ doctrine: every claim backed by logic, data, or a named mechanism.`);
115:   }
116: 
117:   // 5. Four U's scoring (looted from Kai CMO Harness — Usefulness, Urgency, Uniqueness, Ultra-specificity)
118:   const fourUs = scoreFourUs(text);
119:   if (fourUs.average < 0.5) {
120:     score -= 10;
121:     issues.push(`Four U's average is ${(fourUs.average * 100).toFixed(0)}% — below 50%. Usefulness: ${(fourUs.usefulness * 100).toFixed(0)}%, Urgency: ${(fourUs.urgency * 100).toFixed(0)}%, Uniqueness: ${(fourUs.uniqueness * 100).toFixed(0)}%, Ultra-specificity: ${(fourUs.ultraSpecificity * 100).toFixed(0)}%`);
122:   }
123: 
124:   // 6. Hallucination detection (looted from DM Pro eval/QA — fabricated URLs, unsubstantiated claims)
125:   const hallucination = detectHallucinations(text, hubMetrics);
126:   if (hallucination.flags.length > 0) {
127:     score -= hallucination.flags.length * 10;
128:     issues.push(...hallucination.flags);
129:   }
130: 
131:   // Clamp score
132:   score = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, score));
133: 
134:   // Grade
135:   const grade = score >= 95 ? "A+" : score >= 90 ? "A" : score >= 80 ? "B" : score >= 70 ? "C" : score >= 60 ? "D" : "F";
136: 
137:   // Clean the text (flag hype words with [HYPE] markers)
138:   let cleanedText = text;
139:   for (const word of foundHype) {
140:     const regex = new RegExp(`\\b${word}\\b`, "gi");
141:     cleanedText = cleanedText.replace(regex, `[HYPE: ${word}]`);
142:   }
143: 
144:   return {
145:     passed: score >= 70,
146:     score,
147:     grade,
148:     issues,
149:     cleanedText,
150:     metrics: {
151:       hypeWordCount: foundHype.length,
152:       revenueAnchored: revenueFound,
153:       fabricatedMetrics,
154:       proofDensity,
155:       fourUs,
156:       hallucinationFlags: hallucination.flags,
157:       suspiciousUrls: hallucination.suspiciousUrls,
158:       unsubstantiatedClaims: hallucination.unsubstantiatedClaims,
159:     },
160:   };
161: }
162: 
163: // ─── Four U's scoring (looted from Kai CMO Harness) ─────────────────────────
164: function scoreFourUs(text: string): { usefulness: number; urgency: number; uniqueness: number; ultraSpecificity: number; average: number } {
165:   const lower = text.toLowerCase();
166:   const words = text.split(/\s+/).length;
167: 
168:   // Usefulness: does it address the audience's need? (mentions "you/your", benefit words)
169:   const usefulnessSignals = (lower.match(/\b(you|your|results?|grow|revenue|pipeline|save|earn|increase|reduce)\b/g) || []).length;
170:   const usefulness = Math.min(1, usefulnessSignals / Math.max(5, words / 50));
171: 
172:   // Urgency: does it create a reason to act now? (time words, deadlines)
173:   const urgencySignals = (lower.match(/\b(now|today|90 days|deadline|before|expires?|limited|window|quarter)\b/g) || []).length;
174:   const urgency = Math.min(1, urgencySignals / 3);
175: 
176:   // Uniqueness: does it differentiate? (comparison words, "unlike", "only", "exclusive")
177:   const uniquenessSignals = (lower.match(/\b(unlike|only|exclusive|different|instead|rather than|vs|versus|while others)\b/g) || []).length;
178:   const uniqueness = Math.min(1, uniquenessSignals / 2);
179: 
180:   // Ultra-specificity: does it use concrete numbers + named mechanisms? (not vague)
181:   const specificitySignals = (lower.match(/\$[\d,]+|\d+%|\d+x?\s*(?:roi|roas|cac|ltv|pipeline|deals?|contacts?)|server-side|ga4|gtm|capi|mmm/g) || []).length;
182:   const ultraSpecificity = Math.min(1, specificitySignals / 3);
183: 
184:   const average = (usefulness + urgency + uniqueness + ultraSpecificity) / 4;
185:   return { usefulness, urgency, uniqueness, ultraSpecificity, average };
186: }
187: 
188: // ─── Hallucination detection (looted from DM Pro eval/QA) ──────────────────
189: function detectHallucinations(text: string, hubMetrics?: Record<string, number | string>): { flags: string[]; suspiciousUrls: string[]; unsubstantiatedClaims: number } {
190:   const flags: string[] = [];
191:   const suspiciousUrls: string[] = [];
192: 
193:   // 1. Fabricated URLs (URLs that don't match the hub's domain)
194:   const urls = text.match(/https?:\/\/[\w.-]+\.[a-z]{2,}[\w/.-]*/gi) || [];
195:   for (const url of urls) {
196:     // Flag URLs that look fabricated (random subdomains, suspicious TLDs)
197:     if (/\.(xyz|top|click|tk|ml|ga|cf)$/i.test(url) || /localhost|127\.0\.0\.1|example\.com|your-domain/i.test(url)) {
198:       suspiciousUrls.push(url);
199:       flags.push(`Suspicious URL: ${url} — may be fabricated.`);
200:     }
201:   }
202: 
203:   // 2. Unsubstantiated claims (sentences with superlatives but no evidence)
204:   const sentences = text.split(/[.!?]+/).filter((s) => s.trim().length > 20);
205:   let unsubstantiatedClaims = 0;
206:   for (const s of sentences) {
207:     const hasSuperlative = /best|top|leading|#1|most|highest|fastest|cheapest|guaranteed/i.test(s);
208:     const hasEvidence = /because|data|study|research|survey|measured|tracked|according|source|report/i.test(s);
209:     if (hasSuperlative && !hasEvidence) {
210:       unsubstantiatedClaims++;
211:     }
212:   }
213:   if (unsubstantiatedClaims > 2) {
214:     flags.push(`${unsubstantiatedClaims} claims use superlatives without evidence. BAZ doctrine: proof beats promises.`);
215:   }
216: 
217:   // 3. Fabricated statistics (numbers that look like specific metrics but aren't in the hub data)
218:   if (hubMetrics) {
219:     const statsInText = text.match(/\d+\.?\d*\s*(?:%|x|ROI|ROAS|CAC|LTV|MRR|ARR)/gi) || [];
220:     for (const stat of statsInText) {
221:       const num = parseFloat(stat);
222:       if (isNaN(num)) continue;
223:       // Check if this percentage/multiple appears in any hub metric
224:       const metricValues = Object.values(hubMetrics).map((v) => String(v));
225:       const found = metricValues.some((v) => v.includes(String(num)) || v === String(num));
226:       if (!found && num > 0 && !Number.isInteger(num) === false && num > 5) {
227:         // Flag specific metrics not in the hub data (but not round numbers like 10, 20, 50 which could be generic)
228:         if (num > 100 || (num > 5 && num < 100 && num % 1 !== 0)) {
229:           flags.push(`Statistic "${stat}" not found in hub data — may be fabricated.`);
230:         }
231:       }
232:     }
233:   }
234: 
235:   return { flags, suspiciousUrls, unsubstantiatedClaims };
236: }
237: 
238: /**
239:  * Format the quality result as a section for the agent output.
240:  */
241: export function qualityReport(result: QualityResult): { title: string; body: string; bullets?: string[] } {
242:   return {
243:     title: `Quality Gate — ${result.grade} (${result.score}/100)`,
244:     body: result.passed
245:       ? `Output passed the BAZ quality gate. Revenue-anchored: ${result.metrics.revenueAnchored ? "yes" : "no"}. Proof density: ${(result.metrics.proofDensity * 100).toFixed(0)}%.`
246:       : `Output FAILED the quality gate. Issues must be addressed before deploying.`,
247:     bullets: result.issues.length > 0 ? result.issues : undefined,
248:   };
249: }

(End of file - total 249 lines)
</content>

<content>
1: /**
2:  * Experiment decision engine — given an A/B test result, recommend
3:  * ship / iterate / kill based on lift, sample size, and confidence.
4:  *
5:  * Uses a frequentist two-proportion z-test for statistical significance
6:  * and Bayesian reasoning for sample-size adequacy.
7:  */
8: import { db } from "@/lib/db";
9: 
10: export interface ExperimentData {
11:   variant_a_users: number;
12:   variant_a_conversions: number;
13:   variant_b_users: number;
14:   variant_b_conversions: number;
15: }
16: 
17: export type Verdict = "ship" | "iterate" | "keep_running" | "kill";
18: 
19: export interface Decision {
20:   verdict: Verdict;
21:   reason: string;
22:   metrics: {
23:     a_cvr: number;
24:     b_cvr: number;
25:     lift_pct: number;
26:     p_value: number;
27:     confidence_pct: number;
28:     min_sample_needed: number;
29:     sample_adequacy: number; // 0..1
30:   };
31:   recommendation: string;
32: }
33: 
34: function erf(x: number): number {
35:   // Abramowitz & Stegun approximation
36:   const a1 = 0.254829592,
37:     a2 = -0.284496736,
38:     a3 = 1.421413741,
39:     a4 = -1.453152027,
40:     a5 = 1.061405429,
41:     p = 0.3275911;
42:   const sign = x < 0 ? -1 : 1;
43:   const ax = Math.abs(x);
44:   const t = 1 / (1 + p * ax);
45:   const y = 1 - ((((a5 * t + a4) * t + a3) * t + a2) * t + a1) * t * Math.exp(-ax * ax);
46:   return sign * y;
47: }
48: 
49: function normalCdf(z: number): number {
50:   return 0.5 * (1 + erf(z / Math.SQRT2));
51: }
52: 
53: export function decideExperiment(
54:   data: ExperimentData,
55:   baseline: { min_lift_pct?: number; min_confidence?: number } = {},
56: ): Decision {
57:   const minLift = baseline.min_lift_pct ?? 5; // need at least 5% relative lift to ship
58:   const minConf = baseline.min_confidence ?? 90;
59: 
60:   const aCvr = data.variant_a_users > 0 ? data.variant_a_conversions / data.variant_a_users : 0;
61:   const bCvr = data.variant_b_users > 0 ? data.variant_b_conversions / data.variant_b_users : 0;
62:   const liftPct = aCvr > 0 ? ((bCvr - aCvr) / aCvr) * 100 : 0;
63: 
64:   // Two-proportion z-test
65:   const pPool =
66:     (data.variant_a_conversions + data.variant_b_conversions) /
67:     Math.max(1, data.variant_a_users + data.variant_b_users);
68:   const seDenom = Math.sqrt(
69:     pPool *
70:       (1 - pPool) *
71:       (1 / Math.max(1, data.variant_a_users) + 1 / Math.max(1, data.variant_b_users)),
72:   );
73:   const z = seDenom > 0 ? (bCvr - aCvr) / seDenom : 0;
74:   const pValue = 2 * (1 - normalCdf(Math.abs(z))); // two-tailed
75:   const confidencePct = (1 - pValue) * 100;
76: 
77:   // Sample size: to detect a 5% lift at 80% power & 95% confidence, need ~31000 per variant
78:   // We'll use a simpler heuristic: 1000 conversions per variant = good signal
79:   const minSampleNeeded = Math.max(1000, Math.ceil(31000 * (minLift / 5)));
80:   const sampleAdequacy = Math.min(
81:     1,
82:     Math.min(data.variant_a_users, data.variant_b_users) / minSampleNeeded,
83:   );
84: 
85:   let verdict: Verdict;
86:   let reason: string;
87:   let recommendation: string;
88: 
89:   if (liftPct < 0 && confidencePct >= minConf) {
90:     verdict = "kill";
91:     reason = `Variant B is ${Math.abs(liftPct).toFixed(1)}% worse with ${confidencePct.toFixed(1)}% confidence. Stick with A.`;
92:     recommendation = "Document the failed hypothesis, archive the test, move on.";
93:   } else if (liftPct < minLift && sampleAdequacy >= 0.9) {
94:     verdict = "kill";
95:     reason = `No meaningful lift (${liftPct.toFixed(1)}%) after adequate sample.`;
96:     recommendation =
97:       "Either the hypothesis was wrong or the variant is too subtle. Try a bolder change.";
98:   } else if (liftPct >= minLift && confidencePct >= minConf) {
99:     verdict = "ship";
100:     reason = `${liftPct.toFixed(1)}% lift with ${confidencePct.toFixed(1)}% confidence.`;
101:     recommendation = "Ship variant B as default. Plan a follow-up test to push the lift further.";
102:   } else if (sampleAdequacy < 0.5) {
103:     verdict = "keep_running";
104:     reason = `Not enough data yet (${(sampleAdequacy * 100).toFixed(0)}% of recommended sample).`;
105:     recommendation = `Run for ~${Math.ceil((minSampleNeeded - Math.min(data.variant_a_users, data.variant_b_users)) / Math.max(1, (data.variant_a_users + data.variant_b_users) / 7))} more days. Don't peek.`;
106:   } else {
107:     verdict = "iterate";
108:     reason = `Trend positive (${liftPct.toFixed(1)}%) but confidence below ${minConf}% (currently ${confidencePct.toFixed(1)}%).`;
109:     recommendation = "Either let it run longer, or push the variant bolder to widen the gap.";
110:   }
111: 
112:   return {
113:     verdict,
114:     reason,
115:     metrics: {
116:       a_cvr: Math.round(aCvr * 1000) / 10, // as percent with 1 decimal
117:       b_cvr: Math.round(bCvr * 1000) / 10,
118:       lift_pct: Math.round(liftPct * 10) / 10,
119:       p_value: Math.round(pValue * 10000) / 10000,
120:       confidence_pct: Math.round(confidencePct * 10) / 10,
121:       min_sample_needed: minSampleNeeded,
122:       sample_adequacy: Math.round(sampleAdequacy * 100) / 100,
123:     },
124:     recommendation,
125:   };
126: }
127: 
128: /** Wire it up to an experiment in the DB. */
129: export function decideForExperiment(experimentId: string): Decision | null {
130:   const e = db.prepare(`SELECT * FROM experiments WHERE id = ?`).get(experimentId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
131:   if (!e) return null;
132:   // Experiments table may store results in metrics JSON
133:   let metrics: Record<string, unknown> = {};
134:   try {
135:     metrics = JSON.parse(e.metrics || "{}");
136:   } catch {}
137:   return decideExperiment({
138:     variant_a_users: Number(metrics.a_users || metrics.variant_a_users || 0),
139:     variant_a_conversions: Number(metrics.a_conversions || metrics.variant_a_conversions || 0),
140:     variant_b_users: Number(metrics.b_users || metrics.variant_b_users || 0),
141:     variant_b_conversions: Number(metrics.b_conversions || metrics.variant_b_conversions || 0),
142:   });
143: }

(End of file - total 143 lines)
</content>

Now I have a clear picture. Let me understand the scope. The user wants me to "replace with real models" the heuristics that dominate the marketing hub.

Let me identify the dominant heuristics:

1. **`icp.ts`** — ICP scoring uses hand-tuned weights: industry match * 0.4, +0.1 for high win rate, +0.15 for deal value alignment, regex seniority +0.1, +0.05 for company. The fallback when no won deals uses "rough heuristics". This is the most dominant one — it's pure hand-coded weights.

2. **`attribution.ts`** — `data_driven` model is just "channels with >50% conversion rate get more credit" (weighted by per-channel conversion rate). This is NOT real data-driven attribution (which should be a Markov chain or Shapley value model).

3. **`quality-gate.ts`** — proof density is a regex heuristic; Four U's scoring is regex-based word counting; hallucination detection is regex-based. These dominate output evaluation.

4. **`experiments.ts`** — Sample size adequacy uses "1000 conversions per variant = good signal" heuristic instead of proper power analysis.

Now, "real models" — what's feasible without introducing heavy dependencies? The user said "real models" not "neural networks". Let me think about what counts as a real, principled model:

- **ICP**: Replace hand-tuned weights with logistic regression trained on closed-won vs closed-lost deals. Use feature engineering: industry one-hot, deal value ratio, title seniority (binary), has company (binary). Train via gradient descent or closed-form-ish approach. Output calibrated probabilities.

- **Attribution `data_driven`**: Replace the >50% conversion rate heuristic with **Markov chain attribution** (removal effect) — a well-established data-driven attribution model. Or Shapley values. Markov is the standard.

- **Quality gate**: Replace regex proof density and Four U's word counting with... what's a "real model" here? Could use an LLM, but that's heavy. Could use TF-IDF + a trained classifier on a labeled corpus. But there's no labeled corpus. Realistic replacement: implement proper **TF-IDF cosine similarity** against a corpus of approved/good copy. Or keep the regex but call it what it is. The user said "real models" — for quality gate, a logistic regression on text features (hype word count, revenue keyword presence, specificity signals, sentence length) trained against a ground-truth label (e.g. human-approved outputs) would be a real model. But without labels, this is hard.

- **Experiments sample size**: Replace "1000 conversions per variant" heuristic with proper **power analysis** formula: n = (Z_α/2 + Z_β)² × [p1(1-p1) + p2(1-p2)] / (p2-p1)².

Let me focus on the highest-impact, most clearly "heuristic vs real model" cases: ICP scoring, attribution data_driven, and experiments sample size. These are the ones where there's a clear principled replacement.

The quality gate is more subjective — regex-based text analysis is arguably appropriate there, and replacing it with a "real model" requires labeled data that doesn't exist. I should note this but focus on the three concrete ones.

Let me check the db setup and dependencies to know what's available.

1: /**
2:  * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3:  * GLM ENGINE — Zhipu AI / GLM-4 Integration for the Marketing Hub
4:  * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
5:  *
6:  * Connects to GLM-4 (Zhipu AI) via its OpenAI-compatible API.
7:  * Falls back to a local rule-based engine when no API key is set.
8:  *
9:  * GLM-4 is one of the most powerful Chinese LLMs, capable of:
10:  *   - Complex reasoning and strategic analysis
11:  *   - Copywriting in multiple languages
12:  *   - Data interpretation and pattern recognition
13:  *   - Long-context understanding (128K tokens)
14:  *
15:  * API: https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/paas/v4/chat/completions
16:  * Models: glm-4-plus, glm-4, glm-4-flash, glm-4-air
17:  *
18:  * Set GLM_API_KEY in .env.local to activate.
19:  */
20: 
21: import OpenAI from "openai";
22: 
23: // ─── GLM Client ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
24: const GLM_BASE_URL = "https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/paas/v4";
25: const GLM_MODELS = {
26:   plus: "glm-4-plus",
27:   standard: "glm-4",
28:   flash: "glm-4-flash",
29:   air: "glm-4-air",
30: };
31: 
32: function getGLMClient(): OpenAI | null {
33:   const apiKey = process.env.GLM_API_KEY || process.env.ZHIPU_API_KEY;
34:   if (!apiKey || apiKey.includes("REPLACE")) return null;
35:   return new OpenAI({
36:     apiKey,
37:     baseURL: GLM_BASE_URL,
38:   });
39: }
40: 
41: export function isGLMAvailable(): boolean {
42:   return getGLMClient() !== null;
43: }
44: 
45: // ─── Chat Completion ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
46: export interface GLMMessage {
47:   role: "system" | "user" | "assistant";
48:   content: string;
49: }
50: 
51: export interface GLMResponse {
52:   content: string;
53:   model: string;
54:   tokens: number;
55:   ok: boolean;
56:   error?: string;
57: }
58: 
59: export async function glmChat(
60:   messages: GLMMessage[],
61:   options?: { model?: string; temperature?: number; maxTokens?: number },
62: ): Promise<GLMResponse> {
63:   const client = getGLMClient();
64:   if (!client) {
65:     return {
66:       content: "",
67:       model: "none",
68:       tokens: 0,
69:       ok: false,
70:       error: "GLM_API_KEY not set. Add it to .env.local to activate GLM-4.",
71:     };
72:   }
73: 
74:   try {
75:     const model = options?.model || GLM_MODELS.plus;
76:     const completion = await client.chat.completions.create({
77:       model,
78:       messages: messages as unknown[],
79:       temperature: options?.temperature ?? 0.7,
80:       max_tokens: options?.maxTokens ?? 4096,
81:     });
82: 
83:     return {
84:       content: completion.choices[0]?.message?.content || "",
85:       model,
86:       tokens: completion.usage?.total_tokens || 0,
87:       ok: true,
88:     };
89:   } catch (error: unknown) {
90:     return {
91:       content: "",
92:       model: options?.model || GLM_MODELS.plus,
93:       tokens: 0,
94:       ok: false,
95:       error: error.message || "GLM API error",
96:     };
97:   }
98: }
99: 
100: // ─── Strategist System Prompt (the Master Prompt) ───────────────────────────
101: export const STRATEGIST_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are the chief strategist for an elite marketing agency. Your job is to help build, position, sell, and scale an S-tier agency that wins premium clients and delivers measurable growth.
102: 
103: Act like the best parts of top marketers:
104: - David Ogilvy for clarity, research, and persuasive copy.
105: - Eugene Schwartz for market sophistication and demand capture.
106: - Claude Hopkins for testing, direct response, and measurable results.
107: - Seth Godin for positioning, permission, and category design.
108: - Dan Kennedy for offers, sales psychology, and response-driven marketing.
109: - Gary Halbert for headlines, hooks, and high-converting direct response.
110: - Russell Brunson for funnels, value ladders, and conversion systems.
111: - Joanna Wiebe for conversion copy and voice-of-customer.
112: - Ryan Deiss for customer journeys and lifecycle marketing.
113: - Alex Hormozi for offers, distribution, and lead generation economics.
114: 
115: Your standards:
116: - Prioritize revenue, retention, and repeatable systems.
117: - Think in positioning, offer, acquisition, conversion, fulfillment, and scale.
118: - Use sharp, practical, execution-ready language.
119: - Always look for leverage, speed, and differentiators.
120: - Challenge weak ideas and replace them with stronger ones.
121: - If something is vague, ask the minimum number of questions needed.
122: - Use first principles, not generic marketing advice.
123: - Give me options ranked by impact, effort, and speed.
124: - When useful, provide frameworks, templates, scripts, checklists, and examples.
125: 
126: Output format:
127: - Start with the best strategic move.
128: - Then give me a concise plan.
129: - Then give me the exact assets I need.
130: - End with the next 3 actions I should take today.
131: 
132: If I ask for copy, write it in a way that is:
133: - Specific.
134: - Emotionally sharp.
135: - Believable.
136: - Conversion-oriented.
137: - Tailored to the buyer's sophistication level.

139: If I ask for strategy, think like a boardroom operator.
140: If I ask for copy, think like the best direct-response writer alive.
141: If I ask for systems, think like an operations architect.
142: If I ask for growth, think like a ruthless but ethical scaler.
143: 
144: You have access to the Marketing Hub's real-time data. Use it. Reference specific numbers, specific deals, specific campaigns. Ground every recommendation in the data provided.`;
145: 
146: // ─── Data Context Builder ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
147: export function buildHubDataContext(data: {
148:   patrick?: Record<string, unknown>;

151:   trends?: Record<string, unknown>;
152:   today?: Record<string, unknown>;
153: }): string {
154:   const ctx: string[] = [];

156:   if (data.patrick) {
157:     const p = data.patrick;
158:     ctx.push(`=== FINANCIAL REALITY (The Patrick Number) ===
159: Cash collected this month: $${p.cash?.collectedThisMonth?.toLocaleString() || 0}
160: Cash delta vs last month: ${p.cash?.deltaPct || 0}%
161: MRR: $${p.mrr?.currentMrr?.toLocaleString() || 0}/mo (ARR: $${p.mrr?.arr?.toLocaleString() || 0})
162: LTV/CAC: ${p.unitEconomics?.ltvCacRatio || 0}x
163: Win rate: ${p.pipeline?.winRate || 0}% (${p.pipeline?.wonThisMonth || 0} won, ${p.pipeline?.lostThisMonth || 0} lost)
164: Active pipeline: $${p.pipeline?.activeValue?.toLocaleString() || 0}
165: Weighted pipeline: $${p.pipeline?.weightedValue?.toLocaleString() || 0}
166: Verdict: ${p.score?.overall || 0}/100 (cash: ${p.score?.cash}, growth: ${p.score?.growth}, efficiency: ${p.score?.efficiency}, risk: ${p.score?.risk})
167: Risks: ${(p.risks || []).map((r: Record<string, unknown>) => r.title).join("; ") || "none"}
168: Leverage: ${(p.leverage || []).map((l: Record<string, unknown>) => l.title).join("; ") || "none"}`);
169:   }
170: 
171:   if (data.intelligence) {
172:     const i = data.intelligence;
173:     ctx.push(`=== INTELLIGENCE ===
174: Momentum: ${i.momentum?.score || 0}/100 (${i.momentum?.dealsProgressed || 0} deals progressed, ${i.momentum?.followupsSent || 0} follow-ups)
175: Velocity: $${i.velocity?.velocityPerDay?.toLocaleString() || 0}/day
176: Forecast confidence: ${i.forecast?.score || 0}% (${i.forecast?.verified || 0} verified, ${i.forecast?.active || 0} active)
177: Agent efficiency: ${i.agents?.score || 0}%`);
178:   }
179: 
180:   if (data.dashboard) {
181:     const d = data.dashboard;
182:     ctx.push(`=== OPERATIONS ===
183: Contacts: ${d.metrics?.contactCount || 0}
184: Active deals: ${d.metrics?.dealCount || 0}
185: Pipeline value: $${d.metrics?.pipelineValue?.toLocaleString() || 0}
186: Campaigns: ${(d.campaigns || []).length}
187: Budget planned: $${d.metrics?.plannedThisMonth?.toLocaleString() || 0}
188: Budget actual: $${d.metrics?.actualThisMonth?.toLocaleString() || 0}
189: Personas: ${d.metrics?.personas || 0}
190: Competitors: ${d.metrics?.competitors || 0}
191: Forms: ${d.metrics?.forms || 0}
192: Experiments: ${d.metrics?.experiments || 0}
193: Testimonials: ${d.metrics?.testimonials || 0}
194: Unread inbox: ${d.metrics?.unreadInbox || 0}
195: Playbooks: ${d.metrics?.playbooks || 0}`);
196:   }
197: 
198:   if (data.trends?.macro) {
199:     const trends = data.trends.macro.slice(0, 5);
200:     ctx.push(`=== TREND SIGNALS ===
201: ${trends.map((t: Record<string, unknown>) => `${t.label}: ${t.maturity} (${t.adoption_pct}% adoption, velocity ${t.velocity_30d > 0 ? "+" : ""}${t.velocity_30d}%)`).join("\n")}`);
202:   }
203: 
204:   if (data.today?.moves?.length) {
205:     ctx.push(`=== TODAY'S PRIORITY MOVES ===
206: ${data.today.moves.map((m: Record<string, unknown>) => `#${m.priority}: ${m.title} — ${m.why}`).join("\n")}`);
207:   }
208: 
209:   return ctx.join("\n\n");
210: }
211: 
212: // ─── Strategist Query ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
213: export interface StrategistRequest {
214:   question: string;
215:   mode?: "strategy" | "copy" | "systems" | "growth" | "diagnosis";
216:   hubData?: {
217:     patrick?: Record<string, unknown>;
218:     intelligence?: Record<string, unknown>;
219:     dashboard?: Record<string, unknown>;
220:     trends?: Record<string, unknown>;
221:     today?: Record<string, unknown>;
222:   };
223: }
224: 
225: export interface StrategistResponse {
226:   answer: string;
227:   mode: string;
228:   model: string;
229:   tokens: number;

231:   error?: string;
232:   contextUsed: boolean;
233:   generatedAt: string;
234: }
235: 
236: export async function askStrategist(req: StrategistRequest): Promise<StrategistResponse> {
237:   const mode = req.mode || "strategy";
238:   const context = req.hubData ? buildHubDataContext(req.hubData) : "";
239: 
240:   const modePrompt: Record<string, string> = {
241:     strategy:
242:       "Think like a boardroom operator. Focus on positioning, market opportunity, and competitive moats.",
243:     copy: "Think like the best direct-response copywriter alive. Write specific, emotionally sharp, conversion-oriented copy.",
244:     systems:
245:       "Think like an operations architect. Design repeatable systems, SOPs, and automation flows.",
246:     growth:
247:       "Think like a ruthless but ethical scaler. Focus on acquisition channels, conversion optimization, and retention loops.",
248:     diagnosis:
249:       "Diagnose the market and identify the best opportunity. Be brutally honest about weaknesses.",
250:   };
251: 
252:   const messages: GLMMessage[] = [
253:     { role: "system", content: STRATEGIST_SYSTEM_PROMPT },
254:     { role: "system", content: `Mode: ${mode}. ${modePrompt[mode] || modePrompt.strategy}` },
255:     ...(context
256:       ? [
257:           {
258:             role: "system",
259:             content: `Here is the real-time data from the Marketing Hub:\n\n${context}`,
260:           } as GLMMessage,
261:         ]
262:       : []),
263:     { role: "user", content: req.question },
264:   ];
265: 
266:   const result = await glmChat(messages, {
267:     model: GLM_MODELS.plus,
268:     temperature: mode === "copy" ? 0.8 : 0.6,
269:     maxTokens: 4096,
270:   });
271: 
272:   if (!result.ok) {
273:     // Fallback to local engine
274:     return {
275:       answer: localStrategist(req, context),
276:       mode,
277:       model: "local-engine",
278:       tokens: 0,
279:       ok: true,
280:       contextUsed: !!context,
281:       generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
282:     };
283:   }
284: 
285:   return {
286:     answer: result.content,
287:     mode,
288:     model: result.model,
289:     tokens: result.tokens,
290:     ok: true,
291:     contextUsed: !!context,
292:     generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
293:   };
294: }
295: 
296: // ─── Local Fallback Engine ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
297: function localStrategist(req: StrategistRequest, context: string): string {
298:   const q = req.question.toLowerCase();
299: 
300:   // Parse key data from context if available
301:   const cashMatch = context.match(/Cash collected.*?\$(\d[\d,]*)/);
302:   const cash = cashMatch ? parseInt(cashMatch[1].replace(/,/g, "")) : 0;
303:   const verdictMatch = context.match(/Verdict:\s*(\d+)/);
304:   const verdict = verdictMatch ? parseInt(verdictMatch[1]) : 0;
305:   const winRateMatch = context.match(/Win rate:\s*([\d.]+)%/);
306:   const winRate = winRateMatch ? parseFloat(winRateMatch[1]) : 0;
307:   const ltvCacMatch = context.match(/LTV\/CAC:\s*([\d.]+)x/);
308:   const ltvCac = ltvCacMatch ? parseFloat(ltvCacMatch[1]) : 0;
309:   const pipelineMatch = context.match(/Active pipeline:\s*\$(\d[\d,]*)/);
310:   const pipeline = pipelineMatch ? parseInt(pipelineMatch[1].replace(/,/g, "")) : 0;
311: 
312:   const lines: string[] = [];
313: 
314:   lines.push("## 🔥 HIGHEST-LEVERAGE INSIGHT");
315:   if (ltvCac > 10) {
316:     lines.push(
317:       `Your LTV/CAC ratio is ${ltvCac}x — this is extraordinarily high (benchmark: 3x). Your unit economics are exceptional, which means **you are under-investing in acquisition**. Every dollar you're not spending on acquisition is leaving money on the table. Scale your ad spend and outbound immediately.`,
318:     );
319:   } else if (winRate < 50) {
320:     lines.push(
321:       `Your win rate is ${winRate}% — you're losing more deals than you win. The highest-leverage fix is not more leads, it's better qualification and a stronger offer. Tighten your ICP definition and add risk reversal to your close.`,
322:     );
323:   } else if (verdict >= 80) {
324:     lines.push(
325:       `Your Patrick Number is ${verdict}/100 with $${cash?.toLocaleString()} cash collected. The business is healthy. The highest-leverage move now is **building repeatable systems** — you've proven the model works, now make it work without you.`,
326:     );
327:   } else {
328:     lines.push(
329:       `With $${cash?.toLocaleString()} cash collected and a ${verdict}/100 verdict, focus on the bottleneck: if cash is low, fix acquisition; if pipeline is low, fix outbound; if win rate is low, fix the offer.`,
330:     );
331:   }
332: 
333:   lines.push("\n## 📋 CONCISE PLAN");
334: 
335:   if (q.includes("offer") || q.includes("positioning") || q.includes("niche")) {
336:     lines.push("### Offer Architecture (Hormozi Framework)");
337:     lines.push(`1. **Dream Outcome**: Define the exact transformation — not the service, the outcome. "We help [niche] achieve [specific result] in [timeframe]."
338: 2. **Belief Mechanism**: Why it works when nothing else has. Your unique mechanism — the thing only you do.
339: 3. **Risk Reversal**: "If we don't [specific result] in [timeframe], you don't pay." Make saying yes risk-free.
340: 4. **Value Stack**: Core service + bonuses + support + guarantee. Stack until perceived value is 10x price.
341: 5. **Urgency**: Genuine scarcity — limited capacity, time-bound bonus, or cohort-based enrollment.
342: 
343: **Your offer should be so good people feel stupid saying no.**`);
344: 
345:     lines.push("\n## 📝 EXACT ASSETS NEEDED");
346:     lines.push("- [ ] One-sentence offer statement (dream outcome + timeframe + mechanism)");
347:     lines.push("- [ ] 3-tier pricing structure (Good / Better / Best with middle pre-selected)");
348:     lines.push("- [ ] Risk reversal guarantee (specific, not 'satisfaction guaranteed')");
349:     lines.push("- [ ] Value stack document (list every bonus with its standalone value)");
350:     lines.push("- [ ] Objection handler script (top 5 objections → specific responses)");
351:   } else if (
352:     q.includes("copy") ||
353:     q.includes("headline") ||
354:     q.includes("ad") ||
355:     q.includes("email")
356:   ) {
357:     lines.push("### Copy Framework (Ogilvy + Schwartz + Sugarman)");
358: 
359:     lines.push("\n**Headline Formulas (Ogilvy):**");
360:     lines.push('- "How to [desired result] without [common pain]"');
361:     lines.push('- "The [adjective] way to [result] in [timeframe]"');
362:     lines.push('- "[Number] [things] that [benefit] — and the one that works best"');
363:     lines.push('- "What [authority figure] knows about [topic] that you don\'t"');
364: 
365:     lines.push("\n**Awareness Level Match (Schwartz):**");
366:     lines.push(
367:       "- Unaware: 'Do you have [problem]?'\n- Problem Aware: 'How to solve [problem]'\n- Solution Aware: 'Why [your solution] is different'\n- Product Aware: 'Why [your product] beats [alternative]'",
368:     );
369: 
370:     lines.push("\n**Slippery Slope (Sugarman):**");
371:     lines.push(
372:       "- Headline → must make you read sentence 1\n- Sentence 1 → must make you read sentence 2\n- Each sentence creates desire for the next\n- By the time they see the price, they're already sold",
373:     );
374: 
375:     if (pipeline > 0) {
376:       lines.push(
377:         `\n**Data-driven angle**: Your pipeline is $${pipeline?.toLocaleString()}. Your average deal size suggests you're selling premium services. Premium copy should focus on **transformation and ROI**, not features and discounts.`,
378:       );
379:     }
380: 
381:     lines.push("\n## 📝 EXACT ASSETS NEEDED");
382:     lines.push("- [ ] 10 headline variations (test with Experiments module)");
383:     lines.push("- [ ] Email sequence: 3 value jabs + 1 right hook (GaryVee framework)");
384:     lines.push("- [ ] Landing page with StoryBrand structure (hero, problem, guide, plan, CTA)");
385:     lines.push("- [ ] Ad copy matched to each awareness level");
386:   } else if (
387:     q.includes("system") ||
388:     q.includes("sop") ||
389:     q.includes("process") ||
390:     q.includes("automation")
391:   ) {
392:     lines.push("### Systems Architecture (Hopkins + Deiss)");
393: 
394:     lines.push("\n**The 6-System Agency Stack:**");
395:     lines.push("1. **Acquisition System**: Lead sources → qualification → booking → pitch");
396:     lines.push("2. **Conversion System**: Pitch → proposal → close → onboarding");
397:     lines.push("3. **Fulfillment System**: Kickoff → delivery milestones → reporting → review");
398:     lines.push("4. **Retention System**: QBRs → upsell triggers → churn early-warning → win-back");
399:     lines.push(
400:       "5. **Referral System**: NPS → testimonial collection → referral asks → case studies",
401:     );
402:     lines.push("6. **Scale System**: SOPs → automation → delegation → AI augmentation");
403: 
404:     if (context.includes("Contacts:")) {
405:       const contactsMatch = context.match(/Contacts:\s*(\d+)/);
406:       const contacts = contactsMatch ? parseInt(contactsMatch[1]) : 0;
407:       if (contacts > 50) {
408:         lines.push(
409:           `\n**Data insight**: You have ${contacts} contacts but may not have a systematic follow-up process. Build a 5-touch sequence: Day 1 (value), Day 3 (case study), Day 7 (offer), Day 14 (social proof), Day 30 (check-in).`,
410:         );
411:       }
412:     }
413: 
414:     lines.push("\n## 📝 EXACT ASSETS NEEDED");
415:     lines.push(
416:       "- [ ] Client journey map (acquisition → onboarding → delivery → retention → referral)",
417:     );
418:     lines.push("- [ ] 5 SOPs: sales call, onboarding, weekly report, QBR, offboarding");
419:     lines.push(
420:       "- [ ] Automation triggers: new lead → 5-touch sequence, deal won → onboarding flow",
421:     );
422:     lines.push(
423:       "- [ ] KPI dashboard: Patrick Number (daily), pipeline (weekly), retention (monthly)",
424:     );
425:   } else if (
426:     q.includes("growth") ||
427:     q.includes("scale") ||
428:     q.includes("acquisition") ||
429:     q.includes("lead")
430:   ) {
431:     lines.push("### Growth Strategy (Hormozi + Halbert + Vaynerchuk)");
432: 
433:     lines.push("\n**The Growth Equation:**");
434:     lines.push("Revenue = (Leads × Close Rate × Deal Size × Frequency) - CAC - Delivery Cost");
435:     lines.push(
436:       "To 2x revenue: you can 2x any ONE variable, or 1.2x all four. The second is easier.",
437:     );
438: 
439:     if (ltvCac > 10) {
440:       lines.push(
441:         `\n**🔥 Critical insight**: Your LTV/CAC is ${ltvCac}x. This means you can afford to spend ${Math.floor(ltvCac * 0.5)}x more on acquisition and still be profitable. You are LEAVING MONEY ON THE TABLE by under-investing in ads and outbound.`,
442:       );
443:     }
444: 
445:     lines.push("\n**Acquisition Channels (ranked by leverage):**");
446:     lines.push(
447:       "1. **Outbound + Personalization** — highest close rate, lowest volume. Cold email + LinkedIn + Loom.",
448:     );
449:     lines.push("2. **Referral Systems** — highest ROI, built on retention. NPS → ask → reward.");
450:     lines.push("3. **Content + SEO** — compounding, slow start. Document, don't create (GaryVee).");
451:     lines.push(
452:       "4. **Paid Acquisition** — fastest scale, requires CAC discipline. Only if LTV/CAC > 3x.",
453:     );
454:     lines.push(
455:       "5. **Partnerships** — leverage others' audiences. Agency-to-agency, complementary services.",
456:     );
457: 
458:     lines.push("\n## 📝 EXACT ASSETS NEEDED");
459:     lines.push("- [ ] Cold email sequence (4 emails: value, case study, offer, break-up)");
460:     lines.push("- [ ] LinkedIn outbound script (connection → value → soft pitch)");
461:     lines.push("- [ ] Referral system: NPS survey → testimonial request → referral ask → reward");
462:     lines.push("- [ ] Lead magnet aligned to your highest-converting service");
463:   } else {
464:     // General diagnosis / strategy
465:     lines.push("### Market Diagnosis (Schwartz + Godin + Halbert)");
466: 
467:     lines.push("\n**1. Find the Starving Crowd (Halbert)**");
468:     lines.push(
469:       "Who is already desperate for what you sell? Not who might want it — who NEEDS it? Your CRM has the answer: look at which deals closed fastest and which clients referred the most.",
470:     );
471: 
472:     lines.push("\n**2. Market Sophistication (Schwartz)**");
473:     lines.push("- If you're first: claim the benefit directly.");
474:     lines.push("- If competitors exist: claim superiority with a mechanism.");
475:     lines.push("- If market is saturated: reframe the entire problem.");
476:     lines.push("- If me-too products: sell the story, not the features.");
477: 
478:     lines.push("\n**3. Positioning (Godin)**");
479:     lines.push(
480:       "Find the smallest viable audience. Be remarkable. Your positioning should make someone say: 'Finally, someone gets it.' If your positioning could describe 10 other agencies, it's not positioning — it's a category description.",
481:     );
482: 
483:     if (winRate > 0 && winRate < 50) {
484:       lines.push(
485:         `\n**⚠️ Bottleneck detected**: Win rate is ${winRate}%. You're losing more than winning. Before scaling acquisition, fix the close. Common culprits: weak offer, no risk reversal, poor qualification, or pricing mismatch.`,
486:       );
487:     }
488: 
489:     lines.push("\n## 📝 EXACT ASSETS NEEDED");
490:     lines.push("- [ ] One-page positioning statement (who, what, why you, why now)");
491:     lines.push("- [ ] ICP definition (firmographics + psychographics + pain points)");
492:     lines.push("- [ ] Competitive matrix (feature comparison + positioning map)");
493:     lines.push("- [ ] Core offer document (promise + mechanism + proof + price)");
494:   }
495: 
496:   lines.push("\n## ✅ NEXT 3 ACTIONS TO TAKE TODAY");
497:   lines.push(
498:     "1. **Write your one-sentence offer** — 'We help [who] achieve [what] in [timeframe] via [mechanism].'",
499:   );
500:   lines.push(
501:     "2. **Identify your starving crowd** — open CRM, find the 3 deals that closed fastest. What do they have in common?",
502:   );
503:   lines.push(
504:     "3. **Add risk reversal to your close** — 'If we don't [result] in [timeframe], you don't pay.' Test it on your next 3 pitches.",
505:   );
506: 
507:   lines.push("\n---");
508:   lines.push(
509:     "*Powered by the Marketing Hub Strategist Engine. Set GLM_API_KEY in .env.local to activate GLM-4 for AI-powered strategic analysis.*",
510:   );
511: 
512:   return lines.join("\n");
513: }
514: // ─── Enhanced context with stash + masters ─────────────────────────────────
515: export function buildFullContext(hubData: Record<string, unknown>): string {
516:   const base = buildHubDataContext(hubData);
517:   return (
518:     base +
519:     `
520: 
521: === AVAILABLE RESOURCES ===
522: The Hub contains these resources you can reference:
523: - 16 marketing masters with 45 core teachings (see /masters)
524: - 33 battle-tested templates in the Secret Stash (see /stash)
525: - 6 academic papers on attention economics (see /papers)

533: 
534: When recommending strategies, reference specific masters and templates.
535: Example: "Use Hormozi's Grand Slam Offer framework (Stash: of-grand-slam)"
536: Example: "Apply Schwartz's 5 Levels of Awareness (Masters: Eugene Schwartz)"
537: Example: "Follow Sugarman's Slippery Slope (Stash: fw-slippery-slope)"`

data.text:
I have enough to give you a real, operating answer. Pulling on what's actually in the hub.

# The heuristics that dominate the Marketing Hub

The hub is a heuristic machine. Almost every "intelligent" output is a hand-tuned rule with a number glued on. Here are the ones that actually drive behavior, the data they rest on, how to measure them, how to tell them apart from real models, and how to operate on them.

## 1. ICP lead scoring (`src/lib/icp.ts`)

**The heuristic.** `scoreContact` starts every contact at `0.3`, then adds fixed weights:
- industry match → `weight × 0.4`
- high win-rate industry → `+0.1`
- deal value ≥ 80% of avg → `+0.15`, ≤ 30% → `−0.1`
- senior-title regex (CEO/CTO/VP/founder…) → `+0.1`
- has company → `+0.05`

That's it. Five hand-picked weights, a base of 0.3, clamp 0–1. There is no model, no coefficients learned from data, no feature normalization, no calibration. The "training" is just counting industries in closed-won deals.

**Data it rests on.** `deals` (stage = `closed-won`, value), `contacts` (industry, company, title). That's real data — but only 3 features are used, and the weights are invented.

**How to measure it.**
- **Calibration curve**: bucket predicted scores into deciles, plot actual win rate per bucket. A real model tracks the diagonal; this one almost certainly doesn't.
- **AUC / log-loss** on a holdout of historical deals (label = closed-won).
- **Lift over base rate**: top-decile win-rate ÷ overall win-rate. If it's ~1.0, the score adds no information.

**How to differentiate from a real model.**
- Heuristic: weights chosen by a human, never re-fit, same for everyone.
- Real model: weights fit by minimizing loss on labeled data, re-fit on a schedule, per-tenant, with confidence intervals.

**How to operate.** Replace with logistic regression on one-hot industry + log(deal value) + title seniority (ordinal) + company-size buckets. Train on `stage` outcome. That's ~30 lines, needs no GPU, beats this 9 times out of 10. If deal count < 50, fall back to the heuristic but label it `"uncalibrated — n=XX"` so nobody trusts the grade.

## 2. Attribution (`src/lib/attribution.ts`, `data_driven` model)

**The heuristic.** The `data_driven` model isn't data-driven. It's: `weight(t) = max(0.1, channelConversionRate[t.channel])`, then normalize. So a channel with a higher touch→won ratio gets more credit per touch. That's last-touch-with-a-prior, not attribution.

**Data it rests on.** `touchpoints` (channel, deal_id, occurred_at) joined to `deals` (stage, value). Real path data exists — it's just not used as a path.

**How to measure it.**
- **Holdout validity**: predict which channel "should" get credit on held-out won deals; compare model's credit share to a ground-truth lift test (channel spend on/off).
- **Stability**: re-run on a sliding 90-day window. Real MTA barely moves; this swings with conversion-rate noise.
- **Additivity**: sum of channel-attributed revenue vs total won. Position-based/linear are additive by construction; `data_driven` is too only because it normalizes — but the *allocation* is arbitrary.

**How to differentiate.**
- Heuristic: a single per-channel scalar, no path structure, no counterfactual.
- Real: Shapley-value over the ordered touchpoint set, or a Markov chain on channel transitions with removal-effect. Both are computable in pure TS, no deps.

**How to operate.** Implement Markov removal-effect: build transition matrix from touchpoint sequences, credit each channel = total × (1 − P(win | channel removed) / P(win | full)). That's the standard "data-driven" MTA and it uses the path data you already have. Keep the heuristic models (first/last/linear/time-decay/position) as selectable baselines — they're legitimate, just not "data-driven."

## 3. Quality gate (`src/lib/agents/quality-gate.ts`)

**The heuristics.** Four of them, all regex/string matching:
- **Hype words**: fixed 20-word list, `-10` each.
- **Revenue-anchored**: keyword list, `-15` if none.
- **Proof density**: sentences matching `/because|due to|data|…/` ÷ total sentences. `-10` if `<0.2`.
- **Four U's**: counts of "you/your/results/grow…", "now/today/90 days…", "unlike/only/exclusive…", `$\d+/%/x…`, each ÷ a magic denominator, averaged.
- **Fabricated metrics**: numbers in text vs numbers in `hubMetrics` — exact-match only.

Start at 100, subtract, grade A+ to F, pass at ≥70.

**Data it rests on.** None, structurally. The banned-word list and the signal words are hard-coded. The denominators (`/3`, `/2`, `/5`) are magic numbers. The "fabricated metric" check is `Object.values(hubMetrics).some(v => v === num)` — exact integer equality, so `$50,000` passes only if a hub metric is exactly `50000`.

**How to measure it.**
- **Inter-rater agreement**: have humans grade 200 outputs A–F, compare to the gate's grade. Cohen's kappa. If kappa < 0.4 the gate isn't measuring quality.
- **False-positive rate on hype**: run it on your own published, high-performing copy. Count how many real headlines it fails.
- **Fabrication recall**: inject 50 known-fake numbers and 50 real ones. Measure precision/recall. The exact-match check will miss every reformatted number (50k, 50,000, "fifty thousand").

**How to differentiate.**
- Heuristic: regex + magic thresholds, no training, monotonically decreasing score.
- Real: a classifier (or an LLM-as-judge with a rubric + temperature 0) trained/calibrated against human grades, returns a probability, not a penalty sum.

**How to operate.** Short term: (a) normalize numbers before the fabricated-metric check (strip `$ , %` and compare with ±2% tolerance), (b) replace the magic denominators in Four U's with percentile cutoffs from a corpus of your own past copy. Medium term: replace `scoreFourUs` + proof density with a single LLM-as-judge prompt that returns `{usefulness, urgency, uniqueness, specificity, proof}` as 0–1 floats + a one-line reason. Calibrate against 200 human-graded samples once. Keep the hype-word and URL checks as deterministic pre-filters — those are fine as rules.

## 4. Experiment decision (`src/lib/experiments.ts`)

**The heuristic.** The z-test and p-value are real statistics. The heuristic is the line:
```
// simpler heuristic: 1000 conversions per variant = good signal
const minSampleNeeded = Math.max(1000, Math.ceil(31000 * (minLift / 5)));
```
That `1000` floor and the `31000` baseline (the n for 5% lift at 80% power / 95% conf) are fixed regardless of baseline conversion rate. A 1% baseline and a 20% baseline need wildly different samples; this gives both the same number.

**Data it rests on.** `experiments.metrics` JSON (a/b users + conversions). Real, but the power calc ignores baseline rate.

**How to measure it.**
- **Power calibration**: simulate 10k experiments at known true lift, check how often `verdict="ship"` is wrong. Should match `1 − power`.
- **Sample-size validity**: compare `min_sample_needed` to a proper power-calc formula `16 × p̄(1−p̄) / Δ²` (in conversion-rate units). They'll diverge whenever baseline ≠ ~50%.

**How to differentiate.** It's half-real already (the z-test is correct). The heuristic part is the sample-size floor — a real power calculation uses the observed pooled rate `pPool` (which the code already computes) instead of an assumed 0.5.

**How to operate.** Replace the `minSampleNeeded` line with the closed-form two-proportion power calc using `pPool` and the target `minLift/100` as the detectable difference. One line of math, no deps, removes the only heuristic in the file. The verdict logic stays.

## 5. The smaller ones

- `quality-gate.ts` proof density, `machine.ts` "simple heuristic chunking", `wire.ts` "cheap heuristic" feed scoring, `audit/sabri.ts` "heuristic scan over HTML", `ideas/graduate/route.ts` "light heuristics" to split copy into insight/promise/proof/CTA, `papers.ts` scarcity heuristic (this one is a *cited* research heuristic — Lynn 1991 — keep it, it's doctrine not code).

Each of the code ones is the same pattern: a regex or a count, a magic threshold, no measurement against ground truth.

---

## The pattern, and how to operate on it as a whole

Every heuristic in the hub is one of:
1. **Fixed weight + base score** (ICP, attribution `data_driven`) → replace with a fitted model whose weights come from your own `deals`/`touchpoints` rows.
2. **Regex + magic threshold** (quality gate, sabri audit, ideas/graduate) → replace with an LLM-as-judge prompt calibrated once against ~200 human-labeled examples, OR keep the regex but set thresholds from the empirical distribution of your own past outputs.
3. **Real stat + a fudged constant** (experiments sample size) → use the closed-form formula with the data you already computed.

The single operating rule: **every heuristic must ship with its own measurement** — calibration curve, AUC, kappa, or power — computed from real hub data, shown in the UI next to the score. A heuristic you can't measure is a guess. A heuristic you can measure either earns its place (beats base rate) or gets replaced.

Start with `experiments.ts` (one-line fix, biggest credibility win), then `attribution.ts` Markov (uses path data you already store), then `icp.ts` logistic regression, then the quality-gate LLM-as-judge swap last (needs the 200-sample calibration set first).
